title: Pair Addition Match
description: Provided an array of integers `nums` and an integer `target`, return the indexes of the two numbers such that they add up to the `target`. You may assume that each input would have exactly one solution, and you may not use the same element twice. You can return the answer in any order.

title: Ascending Steps
description: You are climbing a staircase. It takes `n` steps to reach the top. Each time you can either climb 1 or 2 steps. In how many distinct ways can you climb to the top?

title: Maximum Mirrored Substring
description: Given a string `s`, return the longest palindromic (mirrored) substring in `s`. A string is palindromic if it reads the same forward and backward.

title: Invert Number Digits
description: Given a signed 32-bit integer `x`, return `x` with its digits reversed. If reversing `x` causes the value to go outside the signed 32-bit integer range, then return 0.

title: Max Unique Substring Length
description: Given a string `s`, find the length of the longest substring without repeating characters.

title: Middle Element of Ordered Arrays
description: Given two sorted arrays `nums1` and `nums2` of size `m` and `n` respectively, return the median of the two sorted arrays. The overall run time complexity should be O(log (m+n)).

title: Pattern String Validator
description: Given an input string `s` and a pattern `p`, implement regular expression matching with support for '.' and '*' where '.' matches any single character and '*' matches zero or more of the preceding element.

title: Maximum Area Bar Chart
description: You are given an integer array `height` of length `n`. There are `n` vertical lines drawn such that the two endpoints of the ith line are (i, 0) and (i, height[i]). Find two lines that together with the x-axis form a container, such that the container contains the most water. Return the maximum amount of water a container can store.

title: Number to Roman Numerals
description: Seven different symbols represent Roman numerals with the following values: I=1, V=5, X=10, L=50, C=100, D=500, M=1000. Given an integer, convert it to a roman numeral.

title: Roman Numerals to Number
description: Roman numerals are represented by seven different symbols: I, V, X, L, C, D and M. Given a roman numeral string, convert it to an integer.

title: Shared Starting Substring
description: Write a function to find the longest common prefix string amongst an array of strings. If there is no common prefix, return an empty string.

title: Triplet Addition Match
description: Given an integer array nums, return all the triplets [nums[i], nums[j], nums[k]] such that i != j, i != k, and j != k, and nums[i] + nums[j] + nums[k] == 0. Notice that the solution set must not contain duplicate triplets.

title: Nearest Triplet Sum
description: Given an integer array nums of length n and an integer target, find three integers in nums such that the sum is closest to target. Return the sum of the three integers. You may assume that each input would have exactly one solution.

title: Keypad String Permutations
description: Given a string containing digits from 2-9 inclusive, return all possible letter combinations that the number could represent. Return the answer in any order. A mapping of digits to letters (just like on the telephone buttons) is given below.

title: Delete Kth Linked List Node
description: Given the head of a linked list, remove the nth node from the end of the list and return its head.

title: Balanced Brackets Checker
description: Given a string s containing just the characters '(', ')', '{', '}', '[' and ']', determine if the input string is valid. An input string is valid if open brackets are closed by the same type of brackets in the correct order.

title: Combine Ordered Linked Lists
description: You are given the heads of two sorted linked lists list1 and list2. Merge the two lists into one sorted list. The list should be made by splicing together the nodes of the first two lists. Return the head of the merged linked list.

title: Create Valid Bracket Pairs
description: Given n pairs of parentheses, write a function to generate all combinations of well-formed parentheses.

title: Combine Multiple Ordered Lists
description: You are given an array of k linked-lists lists, each linked-list is sorted in ascending order. Merge all the linked-lists into one sorted linked-list and return it.

title: Exchange Adjacent Nodes
description: Given a linked list, swap every two adjacent nodes and return its head. You must solve the problem without modifying the values in the list's nodes (i.e., only nodes themselves may be changed.)
